title: Systems and methods for monitoring patient motion during a medical procedure
abstract: Methods of monitoring a medical instrument are provided. The methods may include receiving state information from a control system in communication with the medical instrument; detecting motion of at least a portion of the medical instrument and comparing the motion of the portion of the medical instrument with a threshold motion value that is based on the state information received from the control system. The methods may further include generating a communication message for presentation to an operator of the medical instrument based on the comparison of the motion with the threshold motion value. Corresponding systems are also provided.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of monitoring a medical instalment during a medical procedure involving motion of the medical instrument, the method comprising: receiving state information from a control system in communication with the medical instrument; detecting, by the control system, motion of at least a portion of the medical instrument; and comparing the detected motion of the portion of the medical instrument with a threshold motion value that is based on the state information received from the control system; and providing a system response based on the comparison of the detected motion with the threshold motion value. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the comparing of the detected motion with the threshold motion value comprises at least one of: comparing a magnitude of the detected motion with a threshold motion magnitude value; and comparing a frequency of the detected motion with a threshold motion frequency value. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the system response includes generating a message for presentation in a display system when the detected motion is higher than the threshold motion value. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the message includes a selectable option to request performance of an update of an existing registration. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising: comparing the detected motion of the portion of the medical instrument with a threshold control value, wherein the threshold control value is higher than the threshold 

 motion value; generating, when the detected motion is higher than the threshold motion value, a message for presentation in a display system; and disregarding, when the detected motion is higher than the threshold control value, motion commands received from a master assembly. The method of claim 1, wherein the system response includes disregarding commands received from an operator via a master assembly in communication with the control system based on the comparison of the detected motion with the threshold motion value. The method of claim 1, wherein the state information includes at least one of an indication the medical instrument is in a parked state, an indication the medical instrument is in a drive state, an indication the medical instrument is in a treatment state, and sensor data from a sensor disposed along a kinematic chain associated with the medical instrument. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting motion of the portion of the medical instrument comprises monitoring a change in position of the medical instrument wherein the position is determined using at least one of a fiber optic sensor or at least one electromagnetic sensor. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting motion of the portion of the medical instrument comprises using an image capture device disposed at a distal tip of the medical instrument. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting motion of the portion of the medical instrument comprises: detecting motion by a first motion detection modality; and detecting motion by a second motion detection modality, and the method further comprises comparing motion detected by the first motion detection modality with motion detected by the second motion detection modality. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the threshold motion value comprises a threshold first detection modality value and a threshold second motion detection modality value. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the first motion detection modality comprises a fiber optic shape sensor extending along a length of the medical instrument and the second motion detection modality comprises an image capture device disposed at a distal tip of the medical instrument. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the image capture device captures an image of an interior of an anatomy of a patient, and wherein the control system compares the image of the interior of the anatomy with a virtual view derived from a model of the anatomy. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the state information includes an indication the medical instrument is in a parked state and the threshold motion value is further based on an expected natural motion of a patient undergoing a medical procedure with the medical instrument. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the expected natural motion of the patient is respiratory motion or cardiac motion. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein the state information includes an indication the medical instrument is in a drive state and wherein the threshold motion value is further based on commands received from an operator via a master assembly in communication with the control system. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the threshold motion value comprises an insertion threshold motion value and a threshold lateral motion value. 18. The method of claim 1, wherein: the medical instrument has a measured shape; 

 a portion of the medical instrument extends through a structure with a known shape; and the threshold motion value comprises a threshold motion value associated with the measured shape and a threshold motion value associated with known shape. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the threshold motion value associated with the known shape is less than the threshold motion value associated with the measured shape. 20. A medical system comprising: an elongate medical instrument; a master assembly configured to receive commands from an operator to manipulate the medical instrument; and a control system in communication with the master assembly and the medical instrument, the control system adapted to perform operations comprising: receiving state information; detecting motion of at least a portion of the medical instrument; and comparing the detected motion of the portion of the medical instrument with a threshold motion value that is based on the state information. 21. The medical system of claim 20, further comprising a display for presenting a message based on the comparison of the detected motion with the threshold motion value. 22. The medical system of claim 21, wherein the display includes a selectable option to request performance of an update of an existing registration. 23. The medical system of claim 21, wherein the operations further comprise comparing the detected motion of the portion of the medical instrument with a threshold control value, wherein the threshold control value is higher than the threshold motion value. 24. The medical system of claim 23, wherein: the message is presented when the detected motion is higher than the threshold motion value; and 

 the master assembly is further configured to disregard the commands from the operator when the detected motion is higher than the threshold control value. 25. The medical system of any of claims 20-24, further comprising a first motion detection modality and a second motion detection modality for detecting a motion of at least a portion of the medical instrument. 26. The medical system of claim 25, wherein the threshold motion value comprises a first threshold motion detection value and a second threshold motion detection value. 27. The medical system of claim 25, wherein: the first motion detection modality comprises at least one of an electromagnetic sensor and a fiber optic shape sensor extending along a length of the medical instrument; and the second motion detection modality comprises an image capture device disposed at a distal tip of the medical instrument. 28. The medical system of any of claims 20-24, wherein the medical instrument comprises a position sensor and at least one actuator for controlling the medical instrument. 29. The medical system of claim 28, wherein the position sensor is comprised of at least one of an electromagnetic sensor and an optical fiber shape sensor extending a length of the medical instrument. 30. The medical system of claim 28, wherein detecting motion of the portion of the medical instrument comprises: detecting motion by the position sensor; and detecting motion by the at least one actuator, and the operations further comprise comparing motion detected by the position sensor with motion detected by the at least one actuator. 31. The medical system of any of claims 20-24, wherein: the medical instrument comprises an image capture device disposed at a distal tip of the medical instrument; and the image capture device is configured to capture an image of an interior of an anatomy of a patient. 32. The medical system of claim 31, wherein detecting motion of the portion of the medical instrument comprises comparing the image of the interior of the anatomy with a virtual image derived from a model of the anatomy. 33. The medical system of any of claims 20-24, wherein the state information comprises at least one of an indication of a medical instrument parked state, an indication of a medical instrument drive state, an indication of a medical instrument treatment state, and sensor data from a sensor disposed along a kinematic chain associated with the elongate medical instrument. 34. The medical system of claim 33, wherein the threshold motion value is further based on an expected natural motion of a patient undergoing a medical procedure when the state information indicates the medical instrument is in the parked state. 35. The medical system of claim 33, wherein the threshold motion value is further based on commands received from an operator via the master assembly in communication with the control system when the state information indicates the medical instrument is in the drive state. 36. A machine-readable storage device having instructions encoded thereon, the instructions configured to cause one or more processing devices to perform the method of any of claims 1-19.
